topic: Devstral 2 Review 2026: Mistral Open-Source Coding Agent, 72.2% SWE-bench

key_points: Official Mistral announcement of Devstral 2 (123B) and Devstral Small 2 (24B)
72.2% SWE-bench Verified score for the 123B model
Released alongside Mistral Vibe CLI, a terminal-native open-source coding agent
256K token context window for large codebase handling
API pricing: $0.40/$2.00 per million tokens (input/output)
Devstral 2 is 7x more cost-efficient than Claude Sonnet on real-world tasks
Modified MIT license with revenue restriction for companies over $20M/month
Devstral Small 2 uses Apache 2.0 — truly open source without revenue caps
Requires 4x H100-class GPUs for 123B model inference on-prem
Positions as Claude Code competitor with Mistral Vibe CLI
Cline IDE extension officially endorses Devstral 2 as top open-weights model
Devstral 2 achieves 72.2% and Devstral Small 2 achieves 68.0% on SWE-bench Verified
Optimized specifically for agent frameworks like OpenHands, Cline, and Roo Code
Supports tool use, multi-file edits, failure detection and auto-retry
Both models available via Mistral API and Ollama for local deployment
Step-by-step guide to using Devstral 2 via Mistral API
Explains model architecture: dense transformer vs MoE tradeoffs
Demonstrates multi-file code agent workflows with tool calling
Compares Devstral 2 vs DeepSeek V3.2 (72.2% vs 63.8% SWE-bench)
Covers integration with VS Code via Continue and Cline extensions
Hands-on guide to Mistral Vibe CLI for terminal-based agentic coding
Vibe CLI config.toml allows switching between local models and Mistral API
Auto-approval toggle for tool execution enables fully autonomous coding runs
Available as Zed IDE extension, competing with Cursor and GitHub Copilot
Shows practical examples of multi-file refactoring and bug-fixing tasks

stats.fact: Devstral 2 (123B) achieves 72.2% on SWE-bench Verified, the leading open-weight coding model benchmark
Devstral Small 2 (24B) achieves 68.0% on SWE-bench Verified, running on consumer hardware
Claude Sonnet 4.5 scores 77.2% on SWE-bench Verified, still ahead of Devstral 2 by ~5 points
DeepSeek V3.2 scores 63.8% on SWE-bench Verified, ~8.4 points behind Devstral 2
Devstral 2 is up to 7x more cost-efficient than Claude Sonnet at real-world coding tasks
Devstral 2 API pricing: $0.40 input / $2.00 output per million tokens
Devstral Small 2 API pricing: $0.10 input / $0.30 output per million tokens
Devstral 2 has a 256K token context window, supporting large monorepo ingestion in a single query
Modified MIT license restricts commercial use to companies with less than $20M monthly revenue
Devstral Small 2 is released under Apache 2.0 with no revenue caps or restrictions
The 123B model requires at least 4x H100-class GPUs for comfortable on-prem inference
Devstral Small 2 (24B) runs on consumer hardware: RTX 4090 or Apple Silicon with 32GB RAM
